mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate inversely predicts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as higher rates raise discount factors on future cash flows, reducing equity valuations.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any rolling 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market slack and imminent economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 4.5%, the median 20-day forward return is non-negative or a one-sample t-test against zero fails to reject (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth is positive for two consecutive months and the unemployment rate falls below 4%, the labor market is overheated, which historically precedes Fed tightening that depresses equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS positive for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE < 4%
failure_condition: In months where the trigger condition holds, the 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) and the mean return is not less than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: During a Fed tightening cycle, defined as consecutive interest rate hikes, the negative impact of high federal funds rates on equity valuations is amplified due to cumulative tightening and reduced liquidity expectations.
regime_hint: Fed tightening cycle (consecutive FOMC rate increases)
failure_condition: During periods classified as tightening cycles, the negative rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns is not stronger (i.e., more negative) than during non-tightening regimes (one-sided Wilcoxon test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient (IC) between RSAFS level and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth turns negative year-on-year, it indicates a contraction in the supply side of the economy that portends lower earnings and a bearish S&P 500 outlook over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When INDPRO yoy < 0, the 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak consumer demand (declining real retail sales) combined with high monetary policy rates (FEDFUNDS above 4%) signals stagflationary headwinds that are doubly negative for equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth negative AND FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the 20-day forward return is more than 40% positive or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (positive real GDP growth), declining unemployment indicates strengthening labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During expansion regimes, falling unemployment does not predict positive forward returns in more than 60% of months (binomial test p > 0.10).
